spec_match.rationale: The lab now probes valid <color> inputs (named colors, rgb(), oklch(), currentColor, and var()) plus malformed inputs and non-color properties against the CSS Color 5 grammar.

interactive.rationale: The color picker, native-vs-fallback mode control, live swatch grid, CSS.supports() cards, and failure-mode selector all update rendered output and parser readouts in-page.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page still delivers the feature-index promise of compatibility probes, native contrast-color() rendering when available, and a portable fallback pattern.

edge_cases.rationale: Malformed input, unknown colors, unsupported future-looking syntax, wrong property types, and browsers without native support are surfaced with live parser and fallback behavior.

references.rationale: The see also section links directly to https://chromestatus.com/feature/4841046007742464.

design_system.rationale: Authored styles use shared CSS variables, local button background overrides also set color, and arbitrary user-picked colors are treated as runtime demo data rather than design tokens.

summary: The compatibility lab now clears the major self-heal reference gap and gives a spec-accurate, interactive view of contrast-color() parsing, rendering, fallback, and invalid-declaration behavior.
